description:
Goal
Make the compiler spine data model explicit and coherent. Separate source modules, separately compiled units, linked whole-program IR, and native-lowered IR into distinct named concepts so World no longer reads like an accidental bag of Module values and detached ModulePlans.

Signal
- Each compiler stage has one clear semantic data type and vocabulary.
- ModulePlan is no longer passed around detached from the specific IR module it describes.
- Production callers and docs talk in terms of units, linked program IR, and native-lowered IR rather than ambiguous generic modules.
- Targeted compiler/module/codegen tests and clippy pass after the migration.

Strategy
1. Introduce stage-specific domain types and attach plans to the IR they describe.
2. Migrate pipeline/codegen/World ownership to those types while preserving current behavior.
3. Sweep callers, tests, and docs onto the clarified vocabulary and remove transitional affordances.

References
- src/compiler.rs
- src/modules/pipeline.rs
- src/ir_codegen/compiled.rs
- src/ir_codegen/driver.rs
- .agent/docs/modules.md
- .agent/docs/single-authoritative-plan.md
title: Epic: rationalize compiler world data model
